banco_oraciones.oracion: Ben told Sofía (that) he had lost his phone.
Kenji explained that he was learning Spanish.
Abuela Carmen said (that) she would call later.
Sofía told him she didn't have time.
Kenji said he hadn't seen the wave coming.
Sofía asked Ben if he was okay.
Kenji asked what time the class started.
Abuela Carmen asked whether Ben had found his phone.
Did Ben say he was tired? Yes, he did.
Did Sofía tell him to hurry? No, she didn't.
Did Kenji ask where the market was? Yes, he did.

interaccion: Ben: I lost my phone in the sea. Again.
Sofía: Wait — didn't you **tell me** last month that you'd bought a waterproof case?
Ben: I did **say that**. I just... didn't say I'd actually use it.
Kenji: So when Ben **told us** his phone story, he left out the important part.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: Ben said that he loses his phone last week.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: Ben said that he had lost his phone the week before.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Reported speech retells what someone said without quoting them directly — the verb tense usually moves one step back into the past, and time/place words change too.

filas: Past simple: he said he was tired
Past perfect: he said he had lost it
Past perfect: he said he had seen it
he asked if I was okay (no question mark, no inversion)

ejercicios.pregunta: Sofía asked Ben ___ he was okay.
Kenji asked ___ time the class started.

ejercicios.afirmacion: In reported speech, 'I have lost it' becomes 'he said he had lost it'.
Reported questions keep the question mark and word inversion.
'Tell' needs a person as an object (tell someone), but 'say' doesn't.

ejercicios.texto: Kenji asked what time the class ___ (start).

ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía asked Ben if he was okay
Ben said that he had lost his phone

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Sofía asked Ben was he okay.
ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía asked Ben if he was okay.
Sofía asked Ben if he was okay.

ejercicios.oracion_base: "What time does the class start?" Kenji asked.
ejercicios.respuesta: Kenji asked what time the class started.

ejercicios.pregunta: Did Kenji ask where the market was? (short answer, Yes)

guion: Ben: So the wave just... took it. My phone. Gone.
Sofía: Wait, so what did he say happened?
Kenji: He said a wave had taken his phone while he was swimming.
Abuela Carmen: And did he say if he'd checked the weather first?
Kenji: He said he hadn't. He never does.

comprension.pregunta: What did Kenji say happened to Ben's phone?
opciones: A wave took it while he was swimming
He dropped it in a taxi
He lost it at the market
comprension.respuesta: A wave took it while he was swimming

comprension.pregunta: Did Ben check the weather first?

reading.texto: Sofía wrote in her journal: 'Today Ben told us he had lost his phone in the sea — again. He said a wave had taken it while he was swimming near Cartagena. When I asked if he'd checked the forecast, Kenji said Ben admitted he hadn't. Abuela Carmen just laughed and said she had warned him about swimming with his pockets full. Later, Kenji asked me if I thought Ben would buy a proper waterproof case this time. I told him I doubted it — Ben always says he will, and he never does.'

preguntas.afirmacion: Ben said a wave had taken his phone while he was swimming.
Ben had checked the weather forecast before swimming.
Abuela Carmen said she had warned Ben before.
Sofía believes Ben will definitely buy a waterproof case.
